preview_text:
The model rates Croatia as a heavy favorite with a 79% win probability, leaving Ghana just 3% to emerge victorious. This is a lopsided matchup in statistical terms, with the gap between the two outcomes substantial.

The 79–3 split reflects a match expected to be one-sided rather than competitive. A draw at 19% sits between the poles but is notably less likely than a Croatia victory. The distribution suggests Ghana faces a steep climb and that a decisive result favoring Croatia is the modal outcome.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket differ markedly from the model, both assigning Ghana roughly 17% to win compared to the model's 3% — a gap of 14 percentage points. Both betting sources also credit draws more heavily (around 25% versus 19%). The model sees Croatia as more dominant than the market does; the dashboard does not adjudicate which source is correct.
